title: Macro outlook: jobs, housing, inflation + Dutch farmers protests & EU climate missteps
description:
0:00 Bestie open: Sacks' shopping spree

2:15 Macro outlook: jobs, consumer sentiment, housing, inflation, finding a bottom

39:21 Turkish government finds ~694 million mt of rare earth reserves & EU reclassifies nuclear & natural gas as "green"

48:58 Dutch farmers protests: root causes, how to move forward with smarter legislation

1:09:39 Biden's decline, Bezos responds to Biden's tweet on bringing gas prices down

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the current macro outlook, including jobs, housing, inflation, and the Dutch farmers protests. They also touch on the EU's climate missteps and Biden's decline. The hosts provide their insights on the current state of the economy and politics.

takeaways.text: US job openings are still historically strong
Inflation persists, potentially leading to more restrictive rates
Dutch farmers protests are caused by strict emissions regulations
EU reclassifies nuclear and natural gas as 'green'
Bezos responds to Biden's tweet on gas prices
Turkey discovers 694 million mt of rare earth reserves

quotes.text: US is on the wrong track
quotes.speaker: Unknown

sentiment.notes: The hosts are cautious about the current state of the economy and politics, citing concerns about inflation and the EU's climate policy.
